name: Karaage (Japanese Fried Chicken)
description: Crispy, juicy Japanese fried chicken — thighs marinated in soy, sake, ginger and garlic, coated in potato starch and twice-fried.
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 lb boneless, skin-on chicken thighs, cut into bite-size pieces
Lemon wedges, shredded cabbage, and Kewpie mayo, to serve
recipeInstructions: Marinate the chicken with salt, pepper, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, sake, and sesame oil for 30 minutes.
Coat each piece in a mix of potato starch and flour.
Heat oil to 325F and fry 1.5 min; rest, then fry again at 350F until golden and crisp, ~1 min.
Serve with lemon, shredded cabbage, and rice.
author: Just One Cookbook

ai.cliffNotes: Uniform pieces, a 30-minute marinade, and a double-fry give tender-inside, ultra-crisp karaage.
keyTips: Cut the chicken into uniform 2-inch pieces so everything cooks at the same rate.
Marinate ~30 minutes — long enough for flavor, not so long it over-marinates.
Double-fry: 325F for 90s, rest, then 350F for 45s for a crisp exterior and juicy interior.
